competency_domains.title: Information and data technologies
competency_domains.description: Knowledge and application of methodologies, tools and systems to harness data and drive insight, innovation, and informed decision-making across digital research.

competencies.title: Data engineering
competencies.description: Understanding and implementation of practices for data management, quality, accessibility, stewardship and compliance across data lifecycle.

skills.title: Data modelling and design
skills.description: Ability to plan, architect and implement data systems using appropriate data formats that satisfy research requirements and manage data assets.

skills.title: Data and database management
skills.description: Ability to effectively select a data storage system and store, retrieve, manage and analyse information within it.

skills.title: Data and database query languages
skills.description: Proficiency in data or database query languages for managing and manipulating data.

skills.title: Data acquisition
skills.description: Ability to identify, collect and process data effectively using suitable methods, tools and protocols.

skills.title: Data cleaning
skills.description: Ability to identify, correct and remove errors, inconsistencies and missing values from datasets.

skills.title: Data wrangling
skills.description: Ability to transform raw, messy data into clean, structured formats suitable for analysis, including cleaning, reshaping, enriching, transforming and integrating data from multiple sources.

skills.title: Data curation
skills.description: Ability to steward and enrich data to ensure it is accurate, well-annotated, properly documented, accessible and preserved for long-term reuse.

skills.title: Metadata standards
skills.description: Ability to understand and utilise metadata to ensure data quality, consistency and discoverability.

skills.title: Data protection and ethics
skills.description: Understanding of data protection frameworks and measures to ensure secure and ethical data handling practices.

skills.title: Data lifecycle management
skills.description: Ability to effectively manage data throughout its lifecycle, encompassing planning, collection, organisation, processing, storage, preservation and sharing, ensuring data quality, accessibility, compliance, protection and reuse.

skills.title: Digitisation of text and images
skills.description: Converting physical or analog materials (such as printed documents or photographs) into digital formats making them easier to store, process and share electronically.

skills.title: Computational scientific workflows (data pipelines)
skills.description: Ability to design and implement automated sequences of data processing steps that move and transform data between systems - typically within Workflow Management Systems (WMS) using workflow definition languages.

competencies.title: Data science and analytics
competencies.description: Application of analytical, statistical or machine learning techniques to extract insight and value from complex or large datasets (big data).

skills.title: Data analysis
skills.description: Ability to process, analyse and interpret data using mathematical and statistical methods.

skills.title: Data visualisation
skills.description: Ability to facilitate understanding of data and communicate data analysis findings effectively through visual representations.

skills.title: Data mining
skills.description: Ability to discover patterns and insights from large sets of data, using techniques from statistics, machine learning and database systems.

skills.title: Big data technologies
skills.description: Proficiency in using big data frameworks and methodologies for managing and processing large datasets.

skills.title: AI/ML concepts and theory
skills.description: Ability to understand and apply fundamental concepts underlying AI/ML systems and applications.

skills.title: AI/ML models and technologies
skills.description: Ability to design, build, test, and deploy machine learning models and systems as software applications

competency_domains.title: Software design and development
competency_domains.description: Expertise in design and development of software solutions that are robust, sustainable and fit for purpose, enabling scientific and technological progress.

competencies.title: Web and mobile app development
competencies.description: Design and development of responsive, secure and user-centric applications across web and mobile platforms.

skills.title: General Web technologies and APIs
skills.description: Ability to understand, use and implement fundamental technologies for web development and communication.

skills.title: Web accessibility
skills.description: Ability to design and build websites and web applications that are usable by everyone, including individuals with disabilities.

skills.title: Web frameworks for front-end development
skills.description: Proficiency in using programming tools and libraries to build and maintain the interactive and visual aspects of websites and web applications.

skills.title: Web frameworks for back-end development
skills.description: Ability to use web frameworks to build and maintain server-side applications, including implementing application logic, interacting with databases, and developing APIs for data and service access.

skills.title: Website development
skills.description: Ability to create Web pages or websites to maintain a presence on the World Wide Web.

skills.title: Mobile app development
skills.description: Ability to design, implement and optimise the user interface and user experience of mobile applications, translating design concepts into functional, responsive, and visually engaging app interfaces.

competencies.title: Software engineering
competencies.description: Application of structured approaches, theory and supporting tools to plan, develop, test and maintain high-quality and secure software applications and systems.

skills.title: Compiling code
skills.description: Understanding how compilers translate high-level languages into machine code, including concepts like lexical analysis, parsing and code optimisation.

skills.title: Programming
skills.description: Writing code using the knowledge of programming languages - understanding syntax, algorithms, code and data structures.

skills.title: Generative AI
skills.description: Ability to leverage AI tools to augment and enhance traditional software development processes.

skills.title: Reading and understanding code and documentation
skills.description: Comprehending technical documents to grasp software functionality, architecture and usage.

skills.title: Text mining
skills.description: Ability to design expressions to match specific patterns within textual data.

skills.title: Computer modelling and simulation
skills.description: Ability to develop and use computational models to represent real-world systems and simulate their behaviour under different conditions.

skills.title: Numerical analysis
skills.description: Ability to develop, apply and improve numerical methods to approximate solutions to mathematical problems.

skills.title: Coding conventions, code formatting and linting
skills.description: Understanding and applying coding standards, using code formatting tools and linters to ensure code quality consistency and readability.

skills.title: Software testing
skills.description: Ability to design, execute and analyse various types of tests, covering both manual and automated techniques.

skills.title: Software debugging
skills.description: Ability to use various techniques and tools to identify, isolate and fix errors in software code.

skills.title: Code review
skills.description: Ability to effectively evaluate and improve the quality of code by understanding the code structure, logic as well as soft skills like clear communication and constructive feedback

skills.title: Profiling and optimising code
skills.description: Ability to analyse code execution, identify performance bottlenecks and implement changes to improve efficiency and responsiveness.

skills.title: Log analysis
skills.description: Ability to use log analysis tools, identify patterns and anomalies and interpret log data to diagnose issues and improve system performance.

skills.title: Software version control
skills.description: Ability to use tools and techniques to track and manage changes to source code, ensuring efficient development and collaboration.

skills.title: Software validation and verification
skills.description: Ensuring that a software system meets specifications and fulfills its intended purpose.

skills.title: Good software practices
skills.description: Understanding of and expertise in developing and maintaining software that adheres to the "good" software practices and knowledge of tools that test for them.

skills.title: Documenting software
skills.description: Ability to create clear, concise and comprehensive technical documentation that communicates the design, functionality and usage of a software product.

skills.title: Intellectual Property (IP) and software licensing
skills.description: Understanding Intellectual Property (IP) -  who owns or controls creative work (including software) - and software licensing as the legal mechanism that grants others the right to use, modify or distribute software.

skills.title: Packaging and releasing code
skills.description: Preparing software for distribution, including compiling, bundling dependencies, versioning, preparing release notes and creating installable or deployable artifacts.

skills.title: Deploying code
skills.description: Ability to take packaged software and make it available in target environments, including provisioning infrastructure, configuring services, rolling out updates, managing rollbacks and ensuring minimal downtime.

skills.title: Containerisation
skills.description: Ability to package, deploy and manage applications using containers, enabling consistent and reliable execution across diverse environments.

skills.title: Task automation
skills.description: Expertise in creation and maintenance of automated scripts and workflows that build, test and deploy software.

skills.title: Green computing
skills.description: Understanding of environmentally sustainable computing - environmental consequences of software development and deployment, including energy consumption, resource usage and the overall carbon footprint of the software lifecycle.

skills.title: Parallel programming
skills.description: Applying core parallel software design concepts, methodologies and algorithms for writing parallel code.

skills.title: Programming paradigms
skills.description: Ability to understand and apply different approaches to structuring and organising code.

skills.title: Software patterns
skills.description: Ability to identify, understand and apply well-established, reusable solutions to common problems in software development.

skills.title: APIs
skills.description: Ability design and use Application Programming Interfaces (APIs) that effectively facilitate communication between different software systems.

skills.title: Software requirements
skills.description: Ability to effectively elicit, analyse and document the needs and requirements of stakeholders, translating them into a clear and actionable set of software specifications.

skills.title: Software architecture and design
skills.description: Ability to conceptualise and design the foundational structure of a software system aligned with stakeholder requirements.

competencies.title: User Interface / User Experience (UI/UX) design
competencies.description: Creating intuitive, inclusive and engaging digital experiences through thoughtful design and user-centered practices.

skills.title: Designing user interfaces
skills.description: Proficiency in design principles, best practices, heuristic, tools and understanding of user behaviour for interface design.

skills.title: Designing user experience
skills.description: Understanding user needs through research, creating intuitive interfaces via wireframing and prototyping and ensuring usability through testing.

skills.title: Accessible design
skills.description: Ability to design and develop user interfaces and experiences that are usable by people with a wide range of abilities and disabilities.

competency_domains.title: Computing infrastructures and systems
competency_domains.description: Understanding and oversight of digital infrastructure - encompassing computing, storage and networks - that underpins research, collaboration and digital service delivery.

competencies.title: General systems infrastructure
competencies.description: Broad understanding of digital infrastructure - including hardware, networks and system integration that supports everyday operations and research.

skills.title: Hardware systems
skills.description: Knowledge of the physical components (including CPUs, GPUs, memory and accelerators) that support small and large-scale computational tasks.

skills.title: Storage systems
skills.description: Ability to design, configure, manage and optimise systems used to store, organise, protect, and retrieve digital data, including storage hardware, storage architectures, data management strategies and reliability mechanisms.

skills.title: Network architecture design
skills.description: Ability to design and optimise network structures and topologies to ensure reliable, secure and high-performance communication between systems, considering factors such as scalability, latency, bandwidth and fault tolerance.

skills.title: Network communication protocols
skills.description: Understanding how information is transmitted, routed and received across networks.

skills.title: Network and application security
skills.description: Proficiency in protecting networks, systems and applications from security threats.

skills.title: Systems architecture and infrastructure design
skills.description: Ability to design integrated computing systems by selecting and organising compute, storage, and networking components to meet performance, scalability and reliability requirements.

skills.title: System configuration and administration
skills.description: Ability to configure, manage and maintain systems to support live applications and services.

skills.title: Environment and package management
skills.description: Technical proficiency in sourcing, compiling, building and adminstrating software environments (software packages and dependencies).

competencies.title: Cloud infrastructure
competencies.description: Cloud platforms and computing for flexible, scalable and cost-effective computing, storage, and data services.

skills.title: Cost modelling
skills.description: Ability to estimate, monitor and optimise the cost of cloud resources and services.

skills.title: Serverless computing
skills.description: Proficiency in serverless technologies, understanding of event-driven architectures and a cloud computing execution model that allows one to build and run application code without provisioning or managing servers or back-end infrastructure and allocates machine resources on an as-used basis.

skills.title: Cloud computing
skills.description: Ability to work with cloud services, platforms, and technologies in a cost-efficient and optimised manner.

skills.title: Virtualisation and container infrastructure
skills.description: Ability to create, manage, orchestrate and troubleshoot virtualisation technologies, including the ability to deploy, scale, and monitor applications running within them.

competencies.title: HPC infrastructure
competencies.description: Knowledge of high-performance computing systems and practices that support data- or compute-intensive computational research and large-scale simulations.

skills.title: Supercomputer architecture
skills.description: Understanding of supercomputer architectures (e.g., node types, interconnects, storage systems).

skills.title: High-performance storage systems
skills.description: Ability to design, configure and use storage systems that allow multiple processes or compute nodes to read and write data simultaneously, enabling high-throughput data access in parallel and distributed computing environments.

skills.title: Cluster infrastructure
skills.description: Familiarity with cluster infrastructure's physical components including compute nodes, head nodes, login nodes, interconnects, networking, lock management.

skills.title: HPC software stack
skills.description: Understanding of the layered software environment that enables high-performance computing, including operating systems, compilers, libraries, middleware, runtimes, and scientific applications. This includes configuring, deploying, and maintaining software to support efficient and reproducible computation on HPC systems.

skills.title: Cluster management
skills.description: Managing and maintaining HPC clusters to ensure reliability, performance, and availability. This includes system configuration, resource allocation, user and quota management, security, monitoring, upgrades, and troubleshooting hardware and software issues in multi-user, large-scale computing environments.

skills.title: Job scheduling and resource management
skills.description: Understanding of job schedulers and resource management to allocate compute resources effectively. This includes writing job submission scripts, understanding queue policies, optimising resource requests, and supporting fair and efficient use of shared HPC infrastructure.

skills.title: Performance analysis and optimisation
skills.description: Ability to profile and benchmark applications to identify bottlenecks and optimise applications and systems on HPC systems for high performance.

skills.title: Parallel computing and usage of HPC systems
skills.description: Understaning of cluster operation and proficiency in using HPC infrastructures and running, scaling and optimising parallel applications on high-performance computing systems.

competencies.title: Web infrastructure
competencies.description: Design, deployment, and maintenance of the technologies that support robust, scalable, and secure online platforms and services.

skills.title: Web protocols
skills.description: Ability to understand, implement, and manage the various communication standards that govern data exchange on the Web.

skills.title: Web security
skills.description: Ability to protect websites, Web applications and web infrastructure from various online threats and vulnerabilities.

skills.title: Web and application servers
skills.description: Ability to manage and maintain the infrastructure that hosts websites and Web applications and services.

competency_domains.title: Professional and people skills
competency_domains.description: Core capabilities that enable individuals to operate effectively and ethically within diverse, multidisciplinary and evolving professional environments.

competencies.title: Personal skills
competencies.description: Self-management capabilities such as initiative, adaptability, resilience, time management and a commitment to continuous learning.

skills.title: Analytical skills and problem solving
skills.description: Ability to break down complex problems, evaluate information and available options critically, and select the most effective solution based on logical reasoning.

skills.title: Curiosity
skills.description: Being inquisitive and willing to ask questions, explore new ideas and seek deeper understanding.

skills.title: Positive engagement
skills.description: Demonstrating a genuine interest, energy, enthusiasm and positive attitude toward tasks, learning and collaboration.

skills.title: Adaptability
skills.description: Ability to adjust to new conditions, changes in the environment or unexpected challenges.

skills.title: Resilience
skills.description: Ability to recover from setbacks, cope with stress and maintain motivation and focus in face of difficulties.

skills.title: Business acumen
skills.description: Understanding and application of fundamental business principles to make informed decisions that drive success.

skills.title: Integrity and ethics
skills.description: Being ethical, acting with honesty, fairness and respect and upholding strong moral principles even when it is difficult or inconvenient.

skills.title: Goal-setting
skills.description: Ability to identify, plan and work toward achievable objectives, both short- and long-term, with focus and discipline.

skills.title: Innovation and creativity
skills.description: Ability to think outside the box, generate new ideas and find novel solutions to problems or opportunities.

skills.title: Time management and context switching
skills.description: Ability to effectively prioritise and shift focus between competing demands, manage deadlines and allocate time across multiple responsibilities without sacrificing quality.

skills.title: Maintaining focus
skills.description: Ability to concentrate on a specific task despite distractions.

skills.title: Decision making
skills.description: Ability to assess information, weigh options, consider potential outcomes and choose the best course of action in a given situation.

skills.title: Understanding of mental health, stress and burnout
skills.description: Awareness of emotional well-being, recognising signs of stress or burnout in yourself and others, and applying strategies for maintaining mental health.

skills.title: Self-reflection
skills.description: Ability to conduct reflective practice and identify opportunities for growth and improvement.

competencies.title: Interpersonal skills
competencies.description: Ability to build rapport, demonstrate empathy, navigate social dynamics and communicate effectively in diverse professional settings

skills.title: Cultural awareness and sensitivity
skills.description: Ability to recognise, respect, and appropriately respond to cultural differences in beliefs, behaviors, and communication styles

skills.title: Emotional intelligence, empathy and respect for others
skills.description: Ability to understand, manage and respond to your own emotions and those of others, fostering trust, respect and effective communication.

skills.title: Patience
skills.description: Ability to remaining calm, tolerant and professional during complex or slow processes.

skills.title: EDIA awareness
skills.description: Understanding principles of Equity, Diversity, Inclusion, and Accessibility (EDIA), and actively applying inclusive practices in your work and environment.

skills.title: Recognising stereotypes and unconscious bias
skills.description: Identifying automatic, often unconscious, biases - in oneself, collegues or community - and consciously challenging them to avoid discrimination.

skills.title: Conflict prevention and resolution
skills.description: Ability to identify potential sources of conflict and address them constructively through communication, compromise and mediation.

skills.title: Influencing, persuasion and negotiation
skills.description: Communicating effectively to shape opinions, reach agreements, and achieve mutually beneficial outcomes while respecting others' viewpoints

competencies.title: Teamwork
competencies.description: Working constructively and inclusively with others to achieve shared goals, contribute to group problem-solving and foster a positive team culture.

skills.title: Asking for help or asking to understand
skills.description: Willingness to seek clarification, request support or ask thoughtful questions to enhance understanding and avoid mistakes.

skills.title: Collaboration
skills.description: Working effectively with others, sharing resources and coordinating efforts to achieve shared objectives.

skills.title: Using collaborative tools
skills.description: Ability to effectively use collaborative platforms and tools for sharing ideas, content and real-time collaboration across distances.

skills.title: Understanding, extracting and documenting other people's ideas or knowledge
skills.description: Ability to actively listen, ask clarifying questions and accurately capture and organise another person’s insights or expertise for shared use.

competency_domains.title: Leadership and management
competency_domains.description: The capacity to guide people, projects and organisations with vision and accountability, ensuring strategic alignment, innovation and the effective use of resources.

competencies.title: People and team management
competencies.description: Guiding and supporting individuals and teams to achieve their potential, with attention to wellbeing, performance and professional growth.

skills.title: Team management
skills.description: Ability to lead, coordinate and motivate a group of individuals to achieve common goals.

skills.title: Team performance management
skills.description: Ability to set goals, monitor progress, provide feedback and evaluate performance of team members to improve efficiency and effectiveness.

skills.title: Recruiting people
skills.description: Ability to recruite suitable candidates for a position, including the creation of clear and effective job descriptions that outline roles, responsibilities, qualifications and expectations.

skills.title: Team building and development
skills.description: Ability to bring new people, improve skills, knowledge, and overall capabilities in a team, and foster a positive and collaborative environment that enhances teamwork.

skills.title: Acknowledgement of contributions
skills.description: Acknowledging and appreciating contributions and achievements of team members, reinforcing positive behavior and boosting morale.

skills.title: Shielding colleagues from destructive criticism
skills.description: Protecting team members from unconstructive or harmful feedback by offering support and ensuring criticism is delivered in a constructive, respectful manner.

skills.title: Understanding other people's goals and motivations
skills.description: Ability to empathise with and gain insight into the personal and professional goals of others, allowing for better collaboration, conflict resolution and alignment within teams.

competencies.title: Leadership
competencies.description: Setting direction, inspiring others and making strategic decisions that align people and projects with broader institutional or organisational goals.

skills.title: Long-term strategic planning
skills.description: Ability to do horizon scanning (identify emerging trends, technologies, risks and opportunities and anticipate their impact on research) in order to develop a roadmap for an organisation or initiative's future direction over an extended period of time.

skills.title: Mentoring and coaching
skills.description: Providing guidance, support and advice to a less experienced individual to help them grow personally and professionally.

skills.title: Motivation and inspiration
skills.description: Ability to encourage, energise, and empower others to take action, pursue goals, or strive for continuous improvement

skills.title: Business development and grant proposal writing
skills.description: Understanding the needs and interests of potential funders or decision-makers, articulating the goals and benefits of the proposed project or business case in a clear and persuasive way and providing strong justification for why it should be supported.

skills.title: Setting policies and guidelines
skills.description: Ability to analyse processes, identify areas for improvement and design clear policies and practical guidelines that establish standards, expectations or good practices within a project, organisation or across institutions.

skills.title: Leading change
skills.description: Ability to create a shift in mindset and behavior, empowering individuals and ensuring that the change is sustainable in the long run - drawing on emotional intelligence, strategic thinking, communication and organisational management.

skills.title: Setting a mission
skills.description: Ability to define and articulate a clear mission that expresses the purpose, goals, and intended impact of a project, initiative, team or organisation, providing direction and alignment for decision-making and activities.

skills.title: Starting an organisation
skills.description: Conceive, establish and formally structure a new entity, including defining its mission and scope, setting up governance and operational processes, securing resources, and ensuring legal, financial, and technical foundations are in place

competencies.title: Programme and project management
competencies.description: Planning, coordinating and delivering projects effectively within defined scope, time and resource constraints, while managing risk and quality.

skills.title: Project scoping and setting realistic goals
skills.description: Ability to define and clarify the boundaries, objectives and expected outcomes of a project, ensuring that they are achievable, measurable and aligned with the available resources.

skills.title: Project management
skills.description: Knowledge of the frameworks, processes and best practices that guide how a project is planned, executed, monitored and evaluated.

skills.title: Budget management
skills.description: Ability to plan, allocate, monitor and control financial resources to ensure that activities are delivered within budget while meeting objectives.

skills.title: Task management
skills.description: Ability to effectively identify, break down, organise, assign, prioritise and track tasks and the use of resources (time, money and human resources) to ensure goals are achieved efficiently, within scope, and on schedule.

skills.title: Portfolio management
skills.description: Ability to oversee and manage a programme of work or a collection of projects within an organisation to ensure that they align with the strategic goals, priorities and resource availability.

skills.title: Risk management
skills.description: Ability to identify, assess, mitigate and monitor risks that could negatively affect a project, organisation or process.

skills.title: Crisis and change management
skills.description: Ability to effectively navigate and manage sudden, unexpected situations (crisis) and significant transformations (change) within an organisation or project.

skills.title: Event management
skills.description: Ability to plan, organise and execute events efficiently, ensuring that all aspects are well-coordinated and meet the goals of the event.

skills.title: Stakeholder management
skills.description: Ability to manage expectations of different stakeholders (e.g. partners and PIs), negotiation between partner needs and RSE practicalities.

skills.title: Content and knowledge management
skills.description: Ability to organise, store, share and leverage information and resources within an organisation or community effectively making it easily accessible, well-documented and utilised in decision-making, problem-solving and innovation.

skills.title: Meeting chairing and management
skills.description: Ability to lead and facilitate meetings effectively, ensuring they are well-organised, productive, achieve their objectives and encourage respectful and active participant engagement.

skills.title: Understanding institutional bureaucracy or politics
skills.description: Ability to navigate and make sense of the complex structures, rules, power dynamics and interpersonal relationships within academic institutions.

skills.title: Gathering and understanding stakeholder requirements
skills.description: Ability to actively listen to and engage with users, funders and other stakeholders to identify their needs, expectations and constraints - including asking the right questions, clarifying assumptions and documenting requirements in a way that can guide project planning and delivery.

skills.title: Stakeholder and user engagement
skills.description: Building and maintaining positive relationships with stakeholders or users by communicating clearly, understanding their needs, involving them in the process, gathering feedback and providing value and ensuring satisfaction with the final product or service.

competency_domains.title: Domain expertise and research
competency_domains.description: Disciplinary insight and research acumen, enabling the generation of new knowledge, critical analysis and the application of theory to practice.

competencies.title: Domain knowledge
competencies.description: Deep understanding of the concepts, challenges, and methodologies specific to a particular scientific field

skills.title: Domain-specific background knowledge
skills.description: An understanding of concepts, theories, and best practices within a particular field or discipline.

skills.title: Translating technical language for all stakeholders
skills.description: Translating between technical software engineering concepts and the language of non-software-engineer stakeholders, such as researchers, scientists or other business or academic teams.

skills.title: Adaptability of technical skills across domains
skills.description: Ability to apply scientific, technical or engineering methodologies to different domains and problems.

skills.title: Specialist technical or hardware knowledge
skills.description: Knowledge of specialised computer or hardware systems designed to control specific functions within a larger device or machine

competencies.title: Research skills
competencies.description: Conducting rigorous inquiry, including problem formulation, experimental design, critical analysis and dissemination of findings.

skills.title: Research methodologies
skills.description: Expertise in research methodology - formulating testable predictions based on observations, theories or existing knowledge and systematically evaluating them through empirical methods.

skills.title: Finding, understanding and synthesising relevant information
skills.description: Ability to do literature review - find, read and digest relevant research and synthesise information from multiple sources (such as academic literature, reports, books and research papers) and critically assess its relevance, credibility, and quality.

skills.title: Writing research papers
skills.description: Ability to present research ideas and work in research and scientific publications, including presenting evidence, evaluation and argumentation in academic paper structure.

skills.title: Peer review of research papers
skills.description: Ability to provide constructive, evidence-based feedback on other people’s work to improve quality, foster learning and advance research.

skills.title: Ethical responsibility
skills.description: Ability to identify potential ethical concerns related to research subjects' welfare, data collection, storage and security methods, informed consent and privacy.

skills.title: Legal and regulatory responsibility
skills.description: Understanding of legislation and legal responsibilities when conducting work in specific research areas.

competency_domains.title: Communication
competency_domains.description: Ability to convey ideas, knowledge and impact across diverse audiences, fostering understanding, knowledge transfer and engagement within and beyond the organisation.

competencies.title: Verbal and written communication
competencies.description: Articulating complex ideas clearly and effectively through a range of formats and for varied audiences.

skills.title: Public speaking and presentation
skills.description: Public speaking, presentation and communication with a diverse audience (general public, non-technical, technical or cross-disciplinary audiences).

skills.title: Information synthesis and concise written communication
skills.description: Ability to structure and condense information in a written form for engaging with a diverse audience.

skills.title: Technical writing
skills.description: Ability to create clear, concise, and structured technical documentation.

skills.title: Multimedia and social content production
skills.description: Ability to create, edit, and manage various forms of digital content across multiple media platforms, e.g. graphic design, audio/video/animation production, social media, etc.

skills.title: Note taking
skills.description: Ability to document and organise meeting information and capture clear actions.

skills.title: Active listening
skills.description: Ability to be an attentive and engaged listener.

skills.title: Providing positive and constructive feedback
skills.description: Ability to offer supportive and insightful feedback and review comments on other's work to improve quality, foster learning, and uphold standards.

skills.title: Assertive communication
skills.description: Ability to be assertive in communication, including setting boundaries and declining requests firmly and politely when needed.

competencies.title: Community and outreach
competencies.description: Engaging with wider communities through knowledge sharing, collaboration and public engagement to increase the visibility and impact of work.

skills.title: Peer support and creating safe spaces
skills.description: Providing emotional, social and practical support to other members of a community, fostering mutual understanding, encouragement and personal growth.

skills.title: Networking and relationship building
skills.description: Establishing connections at professional gatherings to exchange knowledge, support and opportunities for growth.

skills.title: Raising awareness of or advocating for issues
skills.description: Identifying, communicating and educating others about a specific problem to generate action, support or change.

skills.title: Community building and leadership
skills.description: Ability to inspire, guide and mobilise people toward a shared vision or a goal.

skills.title: Community governance and management
skills.description: Setting norms, structures and processes to guide decision-making, coordination, communication and participation within a community.

skills.title: Open project culture, structure and practices
skills.description: Understanding of different open science or open source communities of practice and how they work - structures, norms, governance, incentives and sustainability.

skills.title: Community engagement
skills.description: Ability to actively and constructively participate in professional, research and open communities in ways that build relationships, enable collaboration, support others and strengthen shared goals.

competencies.title: Teaching and learning
competencies.description: Designing and delivering effective learning experiences that build skills, transfer knowledge and support capacity and capability development across audiences.

skills.title: Pedagogical skills and theory of learning
skills.description: Ability to design, deliver and evaluate teaching based on an understanding of the theory of how people learn and how best to teach them.

skills.title: Self-learning
skills.description: Ability to learn independently and teach oneself new technical and non-technical skills.

skills.title: Lifelong learning
skills.description: Ongoing, voluntary and self-motivated pursuit of learning for either personal or professional reasons.

skills.title: Knowledge exchange
skills.description: Ability to share information, expertise and good practices within a team, organisation or community.

skills.title: Teaching and training others
skills.description: Ability to convey theoretical and practical knowledge to others, address misconceptions, etc. in training events, while applying good practice from pedagogical theory.

skills.title: Training material development
skills.description: Ability to design and develop various short-format training materials (tutorials, courses, etc.), while applying good practice from pedagogical theory.
